prompt:
The web-prod deploy fails because flyctl can't parse the generated fly.toml. The error is:

  json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field HTTPService.http_service.checks of type []*appconfig.ServiceHTTPCheck

The CUE schema defines checks as a named map (`checks: alive: {...}`) which exports as:
```toml
[http_service.checks.alive]
method = "GET"
path = "/"
```

But flyctl expects checks as an array:
```toml
[[http_service.checks]]
method = "GET"
path = "/"
```

## Fix

Update the CUE schema in schema/fly.cue to define checks as a list instead of a map. Currently (around line 15):
```cue
checks: [string]: #HttpCheck
```

Change to:
```cue
checks?: [...#HttpCheck]
```

Then update all app configs (apps/web/base.cue, apps/flux/base.cue, apps/balance/base.cue) to use list syntax instead of named map:
```cue
checks: [{
    method: "GET"
    path: "/"
    interval: "30s"
    timeout: "5s"
    grace_period: "10s"
}]
```

## Verify

After the change, run:
```
cue export ./apps/web -t prod -e prod --out toml
```

And confirm the output has `[[http_service.checks]]` (array syntax) not `[http_service.checks.alive]` (map syntax).

## Files
- schema/fly.cue — change checks definition
- apps/web/base.cue — update checks syntax
- apps/flux/base.cue — update checks syntax  
- apps/balance/base.cue — update checks syntax

## Acceptance criteria
- `cue export` produces valid fly.toml with array-style checks
- `cue vet` passes for all apps
- CI passes
